Now, the next step in the influence process is think. Here we start privately identifying the invisible gorillas that are naturally present in any influence situation and that can produce a yes. It may seem like an unusual step to take near the beginning of a meeting, but we often see meetings shift into some kind of contest for who has the loudest voice. Again, not a good way to get the best outcomes. So this step involves an individual thinking process, which means the sound level should be zero. When you start using this quiet thinking step up front, it might feel strange at first because we are so used to talking in meetings. But we highly recommend you undertake it as follows. First, give everybody seven minutes thinking time per challenge. Second, always make sure everyone is thinking with the same process in mind, which we have learned from the core motives model and which tells us which of the principles to prioritize in our thinking. Through these principles, we think about a set of solutions based on the activators and amplifiers of each principle. Now you'll learn all about the activators and amplifiers in the upcoming modules.
